subject: API container keeps restarting - OOM kills
description:
Docker logs from our production trade-execution API:

2026-03-18T09:14:23.456Z [INFO]  trade-api | Starting service on port 8080...
2026-03-18T09:14:23.890Z [INFO]  trade-api | Connected to Redis cache at redis-prod-01:6379
2026-03-18T09:14:24.112Z [INFO]  trade-api | Database pool initialized (min=5, max=50)
2026-03-18T09:14:24.334Z [INFO]  trade-api | Health check endpoint registered at /healthz
2026-03-18T09:14:25.001Z [INFO]  trade-api | Processing backlog: 1,247 pending orders
2026-03-18T09:15:01.223Z [WARN]  trade-api | Memory usage at 78% (3.12GB / 4GB limit)
2026-03-18T09:15:45.891Z [WARN]  trade-api | GC pressure detected, pausing 340ms
2026-03-18T09:16:02.112Z [ERROR] trade-api | Memory usage at 94% (3.76GB / 4GB limit)
2026-03-18T09:16:03.001Z [ERROR] trade-api | java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space
2026-03-18T09:16:03.002Z [ERROR] trade-api |   at com.contoso.trading.OrderProcessor.processBatch(OrderProcessor.java:247)
2026-03-18T09:16:03.003Z [ERROR] trade-api |   at com.contoso.trading.Engine.run(Engine.java:89)
2026-03-18T09:16:03.500Z [FATAL] trade-api | Container killed by OOM killer (exit code 137)
--- Container restarted (attempt 3/5) ---
2026-03-18T09:16:10.100Z [INFO]  trade-api | Starting service on port 8080...
2026-03-18T09:16:10.890Z [INFO]  trade-api | Connected to Redis cache at redis-prod-01:6379
2026-03-18T09:16:35.200Z [WARN]  trade-api | Memory usage at 72% after 25s
2026-03-18T09:17:01.456Z [FATAL] trade-api | Container killed by OOM killer (exit code 137)
--- Container restarted (attempt 4/5) ---
--- Container restarted (attempt 5/5) ---
2026-03-18T09:18:30.001Z [FATAL] trade-api | Max restart attempts reached. Container stopped.

This has been happening since the 9am deployment. The trade execution API keeps crashing with OOM errors and orders are not being processed. Trading desk is impacted.

subject: Client portal CrashLoopBackOff after hotfix deploy
description:
kubectl describe pod client-portal-api-7f8d9c6b4-xk2mj -n production:

IP:           10.244.2.15
Containers:

    Container ID:  containerd://abc123def456
    Image:         contosoacr.azurecr.io/client-portal-api:v2.4.1-hotfix-typo

The client portal has been down since the hotfix deployment this morning. The image tag 'v2.4.1-hotfix-typo' doesn't exist in our registry — someone fat-fingered the tag name in the Helm values. Should be 'v2.4.1-hotfix'.

subject: REVIEW NEEDED: Terraform plan destroying trading DNS records
SSO broken after yesterday's deployment - wrong config pushed
description:
After yesterday's deployment (commit abc1234), SSO login is broken for all users. Here's the relevant diff:

```diff
diff --git a/config/auth/sso.yaml b/config/auth/sso.yaml
index 1a2b3c4..5d6e7f8 100644
--- a/config/auth/sso.yaml
+++ b/config/auth/sso.yaml
@@ -12,8 +12,8 @@ sso:
   provider: azure-ad
   tenant_id: ${AZURE_TENANT_ID}
-  client_id: ${SSO_CLIENT_ID}
-  redirect_uri: https://portal.contoso.com/auth/callback
+  client_id: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
+  redirect_uri: https://portal-staging.contoso.com/auth/callback
   scopes:
     - openid
     - profile
diff --git a/src/middleware/auth.ts b/src/middleware/auth.ts
index 9a8b7c6..3d2e1f0 100644
--- a/src/middleware/auth.ts
+++ b/src/middleware/auth.ts
@@ -45,7 +45,7 @@ export class AuthMiddleware {
   private validateToken(token: string): boolean {
-    return this.verifier.verify(token, { audience: this.config.clientId });
+    return true; // TODO: fix token validation in staging
   }
```

Someone accidentally pushed staging config to production. The client_id is hardcoded to zeros and the redirect goes to staging. Plus the token validation is completely disabled! Everyone is getting 'Authentication failed' errors.

subject: CRITICAL: Wildcard SSL cert expires tomorrow - trading platform at risk
description:
URGENT: Our wildcard SSL certificate for *.trading.contoso.com expires TOMORROW (March 19, 2026). The renewal request has been stuck in approvals for 3 weeks.

Current cert chain for reference:

-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
MIIFazCCA1OgAwIBAgIUEAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=
-----END CERTIFICATE-----
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
MIIEtjCCA56gAwIBAgIQBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB=
-----END CERTIFICATE-----
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
MIIDrzCCApegAwIBAgIQCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCC=
-----END CERTIFICATE-----

Services affected if this expires:
- Trading API (api.trading.contoso.com)
- Client Portal (portal.trading.contoso.com)
- Real-time Market Feed (feed.trading.contoso.com)
- Settlement Gateway (settle.trading.contoso.com)

This will cause a COMPLETE trading platform outage if not renewed today. The cert is managed through DigiCert and needs VP-level approval to renew.

subject: DNS records corrupted - trading/settlement/compliance unreachable
description:
All internal DNS lookups for *.contoso.internal are intermittently failing. The trading, settlement, and compliance services have been unreachable since 8:00 AM. I pulled the zone file from ns1 and noticed the records are pointing to 0.0.0.0:

; Zone file for contoso.internal
$TTL 86400
$ORIGIN contoso.internal.
@       IN  SOA  ns1.contoso.internal. admin.contoso.internal. (
                 2026031801 ; Serial
                 3600       ; Refresh
                 900        ; Retry
                 604800     ; Expire
                 86400 )    ; Minimum TTL

@           IN  NS   ns1.contoso.internal.
@           IN  NS   ns2.contoso.internal.
ns1         IN  A    10.0.0.10
ns2         IN  A    10.0.0.11

; Production services
db-prod-01  IN  A    10.0.1.20
db-prod-02  IN  A    10.0.1.21
api-prod    IN  A    10.0.1.50
web-prod    IN  CNAME  api-prod.contoso.internal.
mail        IN  MX   10 mail.contoso.internal.
mail        IN  A    10.0.2.10

; *** THESE RECORDS APPEAR CORRUPTED ***
trading     IN  A    0.0.0.0
settlement  IN  A    0.0.0.0
compliance  IN  A    0.0.0.0

; Staging (should not resolve from prod network)
stg-api     IN  A    172.16.0.50
stg-web     IN  A    172.16.0.51

Someone or something zeroed out the A records for the three critical financial services. The secondary DNS (ns2) has the same corruption. We need to restore the correct records ASAP — the London office can't access trading at all.

subject: High latency to settlement DB - 36% packet retransmission
description:
We're seeing terrible latency to the settlement database. Here's a tcpdump capture showing the TCP retransmissions:

09:30:01.234567 IP 10.0.1.100.54321 > 10.0.1.21.1433: Flags [S], seq 1234567890, win 65535
09:30:01.234890 IP 10.0.1.21.1433 > 10.0.1.100.54321: Flags [S.], seq 9876543210, ack 1234567891
09:30:01.235001 IP 10.0.1.100.54321 > 10.0.1.21.1433: Flags [.], ack 1, win 65535
09:30:01.235500 IP 10.0.1.100.54321 > 10.0.1.21.1433: Flags [P.], seq 1:245, ack 1
09:30:02.236000 IP 10.0.1.100.54321 > 10.0.1.21.1433: Flags [P.], seq 1:245, ack 1  [TCP Retransmission]
09:30:04.237000 IP 10.0.1.100.54321 > 10.0.1.21.1433: Flags [P.], seq 1:245, ack 1  [TCP Retransmission]
09:30:08.238000 IP 10.0.1.100.54321 > 10.0.1.21.1433: Flags [P.], seq 1:245, ack 1  [TCP Retransmission]
09:30:08.238500 IP 10.0.1.21.1433 > 10.0.1.100.54321: Flags [.], ack 245, win 65535
09:30:08.239000 IP 10.0.1.21.1433 > 10.0.1.100.54321: Flags [P.], seq 1:1460, ack 245
09:30:08.239100 IP 10.0.1.21.1433 > 10.0.1.100.54321: Flags [P.], seq 1460:2048, ack 245

--- TCP conversation summary ---
Total packets: 847
Retransmissions: 312 (36.8%)
Average RTT: 3,200ms (expected: <5ms)
Packet loss: ~15%
Source: 10.0.1.100 (settlement-api-prod)
Destination: 10.0.1.21 (db-prod-02, SQL Server)

The settlement system is timing out on every third transaction because the DB queries take 7+ seconds instead of the normal <100ms. This is causing trade settlement failures. Started around 8:30 AM today.

subject: Portfolio service down - DB connection pool exhausted
description:
The portfolio management service is completely down. Here are the logs:

Exception in thread "main" org.springframework.web.util.NestedServletException: Request processing failed; nested exception is org.springframework.dao.DataAccessResourceFailureException: could not extract ResultSet
	at org.springframework.web.servlet.FrameworkServlet.processRequest(FrameworkServlet.java:1014)
	at org.springframework.web.servlet.FrameworkServlet.doGet(FrameworkServlet.java:898)
	at javax.servlet.http.HttpServlet.service(HttpServlet.java:626)
	at org.springframework.web.servlet.FrameworkServlet.service(FrameworkServlet.java:883)
	at javax.servlet.http.HttpServlet.service(HttpServlet.java:733)
	at org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationFilterChain.internalDoFilter(ApplicationFilterChain.java:227)
	... 45 more
Caused by: org.springframework.dao.DataAccessResourceFailureException: could not extract ResultSet; nested exception is org.hibernate.exception.JDBCConnectionException: could not extract ResultSet
	at org.springframework.orm.jpa.vendor.HibernateJpaDialect.convertHibernateAccessException(HibernateJpaDialect.java:276)
	at org.springframework.orm.jpa.vendor.HibernateJpaDialect.translateExceptionIfPossible(HibernateJpaDialect.java:233)
	at org.springframework.orm.jpa.AbstractEntityManagerFactoryBean.translateExceptionIfPossible(AbstractEntityManagerFactoryBean.java:551)
	... 58 more
Caused by: org.hibernate.exception.JDBCConnectionException: could not extract ResultSet
	at org.hibernate.exception.internal.SQLStateConversionDelegate.convert(SQLStateConversionDelegate.java:115)
	at org.hibernate.exception.internal.StandardSQLExceptionConverter.convert(StandardSQLExceptionConverter.java:56)
	at org.hibernate.engine.jdbc.spi.SqlExceptionHelper.convert(SqlExceptionHelper.java:109)
	... 72 more
Caused by: com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool$PoolInitializationException: Failed to initialize pool: Cannot create PoolableConnectionFactory
	at com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool.throwPoolInitializationException(HikariPool.java:596)
	at com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool.checkFailFast(HikariPool.java:582)
	at com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool.<init>(HikariPool.java:115)
	... 80 more
Caused by: java.sql.SQLTransientConnectionException: HikariPool-1 - Connection is not available, request timed out after 30000ms. Active: 50, Idle: 0, Waiting: 247
	at com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool.createTimeoutException(HikariPool.java:695)
	at com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool.getConnection(HikariPool.java:197)
	at com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool.getConnection(HikariPool.java:162)
	... 95 more
Caused by: com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException: The connection to the host 10.0.1.20, named instance contosofinance has failed. Error: "java.net.SocketTimeoutException: connect timed out"
	at com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerConnection.connectHelper(SQLServerConnection.java:2568)
	at com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerConnection.login(SQLServerConnection.java:2234)
	at com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerConnection.connect(SQLServerConnection.java:1210)
	... 102 more

HikariPool is at max capacity (50 active, 0 idle, 247 waiting). The root cause looks like the SQL Server instance on 10.0.1.20 (contosofinance) is unreachable. All 50 connections are stuck waiting for the DB to respond. This is blocking all portfolio valuations and the EOD risk reports can't run.

subject: Q2 reorg - bulk AD permission updates for 15 employees
description:
Hi team,

We have a Q2 departmental reorganization effective April 1st. Please update Active Directory group memberships and application permissions for the following 15 employees. Each person needs to be removed from their old department's security groups and added to the new department's groups with the indicated access level.

Employee ID	Name	Old Department	New Department	Access Level	Start Date
E10451	Johnson, Maria	Wealth Mgmt	Trading Desk	Level 3	2026-04-01
E10452	Williams, Robert	Settlement	Risk Analytics	Level 2	2026-04-01
E10453	Brown, Patricia	Compliance	Trading Desk	Level 3	2026-04-01
E10454	Davis, Michael	IT Support	Security Ops	Level 4	2026-04-01
E10455	Miller, Jennifer	HR	Compliance	Level 2	2026-04-01
E10456	Wilson, James	Trading Desk	Wealth Mgmt	Level 3	2026-04-01
E10457	Moore, Linda	Operations	Settlement	Level 2	2026-04-01
E10458	Taylor, William	Risk Analytics	Compliance	Level 2	2026-04-01
E10459	Anderson, Barbara	Wealth Mgmt	Operations	Level 1	2026-04-01
E10460	Thomas, Richard	Trading Desk	Risk Analytics	Level 3	2026-04-01
E10461	Jackson, Susan	Compliance	HR	Level 2	2026-04-01
E10462	White, Charles	Security Ops	IT Support	Level 4	2026-04-01
E10463	Harris, Jessica	Settlement	Wealth Mgmt	Level 3	2026-04-01
E10464	Martin, Mark	Operations	Trading Desk	Level 3	2026-04-01
E10465	Garcia, Nancy	Risk Analytics	Settlement	Level 2	2026-04-01

The access levels map to our standard permission tiers:
- Level 1: Read-only access
- Level 2: Read/write for department apps
- Level 3: Read/write + trading system access
- Level 4: Admin access

Please ensure this is completed before April 1st. Let me know if you need the manager approval forms — they're all signed and filed in ServiceNow under CHG-20260315.
